findings.title: Admin API has zero authentication
findings.claim_contradicted: Admin only endpoint with access control
findings.description: GET /api/v1/admin/quarantine and POST /api/v1/admin/quarantine/{id}/approve have no authentication dependency. Anyone on the internet can access these endpoints.
findings.evidence: admin.py L9-41: No Depends(verify_admin) or any auth middleware
findings.user_impact: Critical - any actor can view quarantine list and release quarantined data

findings.title: Chart API returns hardcoded Mock data
findings.claim_contradicted: Time-Series API using actual PriceHistory from DB
findings.description: chart.py returns hardcoded 3 data points for H100 Vast.ai regardless of query params. No DB query whatsoever.
findings.evidence: chart.py L33-43: ChartDataPoint hardcoded dates 2026-07-20 to 2026-07-22 with fixed prices
findings.user_impact: Critical - users see fake price chart data, never updated
findings.data_integrity_impact: Critical - fake data presented as real market data

findings.title: PostgresStorage.save() is not implemented — only logs
findings.description: PostgresStorage.save() contains only a logger.info() call. No SQLAlchemy session, no INSERT statement. When USE_REAL_DB=True, collected data is silently discarded.
findings.evidence: storage.py L28-32: logger.info() is the entire implementation
findings.user_impact: Critical - all collected data lost when USE_REAL_DB=True

findings.title: AWS Crawler returns hardcoded Mock data
findings.claim_contradicted: AWS EC2/Spot API crawler collecting real data
findings.description: fetch_raw_data() returns a hardcoded dict with one SKU. Always produces price_per_hour=32.77 regardless of actual AWS prices.
findings.evidence: aws.py L9-12: 'Scaffold for Phase 15' comment, return hardcoded product dict

findings.title: Outbox Publisher/Consumer completely absent
findings.claim_contradicted: Transactional Outbox Pattern - zero data loss
findings.description: PostgresOutboxEventBus class exists but is never called during PriceObservation save. No publisher worker exists to consume outbox_events and publish to message broker.
findings.evidence: No code in orchestrator.py or any save path calls event_bus.publish(). No worker process reads outbox_events.

findings.title: Celery Beat task name mismatch — scheduler broken
findings.description: celery_app.py registers task 'orchestrator.run_all_collections' in beat_schedule. Actual task is named 'orchestrator.tick'. Celery Beat will fail with NotRegistered exception on every tick.

findings.title: History API always returns empty array
findings.description: GET /api/v1/history/{offering_id} always returns {"history": []}. TODO comment, no DB query.
findings.evidence: history.py L18-19: # TODO: Implement history logic → return {"offering_id": offering_id, "history": []}

findings.title: Chat API is Mock Scaffold
findings.description: Always returns 'This NLP feature is currently in scaffolding phase' regardless of input.
findings.evidence: chat.py L27-33: TODO comment and mock ChatResponse

findings.title: Wildcard CORS is production default
findings.description: BACKEND_CORS_ORIGINS defaults to ["*"]. If production deploys without explicit env override, any origin can make credentialed requests.
findings.security_impact: High - CSRF if cookies used, cross-origin API abuse

findings.title: Hardcoded default DB password 'password'
findings.description: POSTGRES_PASSWORD defaults to literal string 'password'. Docker Compose also uses 'password'. Production deployment without env override will use insecure password.
findings.evidence: config.py L14: POSTGRES_PASSWORD: str = "password". docker-compose.yml L8: POSTGRES_PASSWORD: password

findings.title: QuarantineService disconnected from pipeline
findings.claim_contradicted: Data quality isolation with quarantine severity
findings.description: QuarantineService.inspect() exists but is never called in orchestrator.py or any collection pipeline. Negative prices and extreme prices flow through unchecked.
findings.additional_bug: QuarantineService checks 'hourly_price' key but crawlers produce 'price_per_hour' — key mismatch means quarantine would always pass even if connected

findings.title: IdempotencyKey never used in actual tasks
findings.description: acquire_lock() function implemented but never imported or called in orchestrator.py. Same job can run multiple times without prevention.

findings.title: CircuitBreaker not connected to crawlers
findings.claim_contradicted: Circuit breaker for provider fault isolation
findings.description: CircuitBreaker class exists but no crawler imports or uses it. Provider failures will not be isolated. Also: in-memory state reset on restart.

findings.title: Rate Limiter is in-memory — ineffective in distributed deployment
findings.description: slowapi with get_remote_address uses in-memory storage by default. Multiple API pods will each have independent rate limit counters. A user can exceed the per-minute limit N times (once per pod).

findings.title: Health Check references undefined CELERY_BROKER_URL
findings.description: health.py uses settings.CELERY_BROKER_URL but config.py only defines REDIS_URL. AttributeError will crash health endpoint.
findings.evidence: health.py L37: redis.from_url(settings.CELERY_BROKER_URL). config.py has no CELERY_BROKER_URL field.

findings.title: Terraform: RDS lacks subnet group, deletion protection, encryption
findings.description: RDS instance has skip_final_snapshot=true, no deletion_protection, no db_subnet_group_name (will use default public VPC), no storage_encrypted. Data at risk.

findings.title: Render Blueprint missing Celery Beat scheduler service
findings.description: render.yaml defines web and worker services but no Celery Beat scheduler. Without Beat, no periodic task dispatching occurs. All collection is manual-only.

findings.title: Render Blueprint on free plan — production unacceptable
findings.description: Both web and worker services use 'plan: free'. Render free tier spins down after inactivity, inadequate for production data collection.

findings.title: Search does not exclude quarantined prices
findings.description: Search query joins PricingPlan → PriceObservation but does not filter by quality status. Quarantined observations can appear in search results.

findings.title: price_history uses Float for price — floating point precision risk
findings.description: price_per_hour: Mapped[float] = mapped_column(Float) — financial data should use Numeric/Decimal. Float can cause $0.00000001 precision errors in aggregations.

findings.title: USE_REAL_DB defaults to False — silent data loss risk in production
findings.description: Feature flag defaults to False, meaning all collected data goes to local JSON files even if a production DB is configured. A deployment without explicit USE_REAL_DB=True env var silently loses all data.

findings.title: PriceHistory table has no writer
findings.description: PriceHistory model and migration exist. No code anywhere inserts into this table. Chart and historical analysis will always show empty data.

findings.title: CollectionRun records never created
findings.description: orchestrator.py never creates a CollectionRun record. DataQualityIssue FK to collection_runs.id will fail if quarantine service was connected.

findings.title: ScheduleConfig table never read by scheduler
findings.description: ScheduleConfig model and DB table exist. celery_app.py uses hardcoded crontab, never reads this table for dynamic scheduling.

findings.title: QuarantineService key mismatch with actual data schema
findings.description: QuarantineService checks item.get('hourly_price') but VastCrawler produces 'price_per_hour'. Even if connected, quarantine rules would never trigger.

findings.title: SSE: one DB session per long-lived SSE connection — potential leak
findings.description: event_generator() holds AsyncSession for the duration of the SSE connection. 100 concurrent SSE connections = 100 DB sessions held open indefinitely.

findings.title: backup_db.sh S3 upload commented out — no offsite backup
findings.description: S3 upload is commented: '# aws s3 cp ...'. Local-only backup provides no disaster recovery if the DB server fails.

findings.title: Vast.ai API endpoint without authentication — may violate ToS
findings.description: Crawling https://console.vast.ai/api/v0/bundles/ without authentication. Vast.ai ToS and data redistribution terms need legal review before public display.

findings.title: BaseProviderAdapter dead code — never used
findings.description: Two abstract base classes exist (BaseProviderCrawler and BaseProviderAdapter). Only BaseProviderCrawler is used. base_adapter.py is dead code.

findings.title: Test coverage: only 3 test files, trivial assertions
findings.description: test_api.py: asserts status_code in [200, 429] (allows any response including 500). test_crawlers.py: only checks hasattr (existence, not behavior). No integration tests, no migration tests, no E2E tests.
